# Steward

> Steward is an open-source Linux runtime for admitting and executing AI agents.
> A sandbox limits untrusted code but does not identify the authorizing tenant or
> authorize one exact external effect. Steward adds signed local policy,
> connector-scoped Authorized Effects keys, policy-enforced multi-party
> exact-request permits,
> Docker/gVisor enforcement, spend-before-DNS records, and offline-verifiable
> receipts without
> requiring a hosted service. A signed agent release can also bind one qualified
> outcome to an exact offline archive, deterministic canary, evidence digest, and
> known limits without granting runtime authority. Steward includes an optional
> self-hosted controller while keeping the node contracts independently implementable.
> An operator-side coordinator can promote one qualified Hermes release through an
> explicit first-node canary and later batches without giving the controller either
> private signing key, then verify the complete proof set offline.

Documentation scope: current supported capabilities
License: Apache-2.0
Source: https://github.com/hardrails/steward
Documentation: https://hardrails.github.io/steward/

## Start here

- [Overview](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/): Purpose, enforced boundary, and documentation map
- [Install](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/getting-started/): Interactive and unattended Linux installation
- [Operate Steward Control](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/control-plane/): Install the bundled controller, create tenants and operators, enroll nodes, deliver signed commands, and protect durable state
- [Embedded operator console](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/operator-console/): Inspect summary, attention, nodes, command metadata, and credential metadata through the read-only `/console/` surface
- [Why Steward exists](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/product/positioning/): Operator problem, authorization path, and receipt limits
- [Market analysis](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/product/market-analysis/): Dated competitive matrix and research signals
- [Limitations](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/limitations/): Current capabilities, exclusions, and trusted components
- [FAQ](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/faq/): Common product and operational questions

## How-to guides

- [Offline agent catalog](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/agent-catalog/): Issue and verify a curator-signed descriptive catalog, search and compare exact signed capsule metadata, transfer it across an air gap, and pin accepted revisions
- [Proof-carrying agent activation](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/agent-activation/): Verify a signed outcome-led Hermes release and exact archive, keep task signing off-node, run the deterministic canary, and retain an offline-verifiable evidence set
- [Proof-carrying fleet rollout](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/fleet-rollout/): Authorize one exact plan, promote a qualified Hermes release through an explicit canary and signed evidence-bound batches, resume from crash-recoverable immutable artifacts, and verify an offline proof digest that commits the exact plan, promotions, and signed outer command envelopes
- [Operator console](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/operator-console/): Use the same scoped control API bearer in an embedded, air-gapped, read-only React interface; keep browser extensions inside the documented trust boundary
- [Workload lifecycle](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/workload-lifecycle/): Use the host-local Executor API
- [Signed admission](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/signed-admission/): Sign capsules and policy, configure fences and receipts, verify offline
- [Positive capabilities](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/positive-capabilities/): Configure optional state, inference, service, connector, and HTTP(S) egress grants
- [Authorized Effects](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/authorized-effects/): Assume the agent is compromised and require a signed, exact, one-use permit before a Steward-mediated connector effect
- [Gateway credentials](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/secrets/): Materialize inference keys and connector tokens from OpenBao or another trusted service into owner-only Gateway files without exposing reusable values to agents
- [Tenant-signed service tasks](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/hermes-agent/#authorize-and-run-one-exact-hermes-task): Scope a tenant key to Hermes, issue one exact task bundle, dispatch it through loopback Gateway, and audit its receipts
- [Authenticated connectors](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/connectors/): Broker exact API operations without directly configuring the workload with upstream credentials or origins; optionally require a tenant-scoped signature over one exact request; verify permit-to-receipt linkage offline
- [Signed egress](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/egress/): Configure HTTP(S) routes, private CIDRs, audit, and diagnostics
- [Terraform](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/terraform/): Non-secret cloud bootstrap and AWS hardening
- [MCP](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/mcp/): Operate the fleet controller, a local node, or both with Model Context Protocol 2025-11-25 stdio tools
- [Hermes Agent](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/hermes-agent/): Build the exact-pinned qualified adapter; review its gVisor qualification evidence, signed workspace and connector skills, service API, and limits
- [OpenClaw](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/openclaw/): Review the layout contract, bounded WebSocket path, and adapter acceptance gate
- [Air-gapped installation](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/air-gapped/): Offline import and verification
- [Upgrade and rollback](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/upgrades/): Manifest-bound activation, drained-node requirements, durable-format checks, and rollback limits

## Explanation

- [Architecture](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/architecture/): Process and system boundaries
- [Security model](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/security-model/): Trust, isolation, and residual risks
- [Control-plane boundary](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/control-plane-boundary/): Responsibilities kept separate between Steward Control and each node

## Reference

- [Platform support](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/platform-support/): Supported hosts and artifacts
- [Configuration](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/configuration/): Flags, defaults, paths, and validation
- [APIs](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/api/): Authoritative OpenAPI contracts
- [Catalog, agent release, image, permit, task, and evidence tools](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/offline-tools/): Issue and verify curator catalogs and signed outcome releases; search and compare exact capsule metadata; verify and import Open Container Initiative (OCI) archives; issue and audit exact connector and service-task permits; dispatch a qualified Hermes run; verify receipt chains
- [Release artifacts](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/release-artifacts/): Filenames and checksum verification

## Important capability boundary

On a dedicated single-tenant host, Steward can grant persistent state across
approved workload replacements; the Docker volume has no enforced byte or inode
quota and is disabled on a shared host. Steward can also grant OpenAI-compatible
inference without directly configuring or injecting the upstream credential into
the workload, one private service, and named credential-brokered API operations with
spend-before-effect call budgets. A connector can additionally require a short-lived
permit signed by a tenant-scoped off-node key for the exact admitted operation,
task, and request bytes. Authorized Effects can make that boundary mandatory in
signed tenant policy, prohibit generic egress, require a version-2 or multi-party
version-3 permit, spend it before DNS, and record format-5 or format-6 evidence. A
service-scoped tenant task key can
separately authorize one exact JSON request to a configured agent-service operation.
Gateway writes receipt
format 4 across authorization, dispatch, and terminal observation, does not record the raw prompt, and returns a durable run
ID on an exact successful replay without dispatching again. This is node-local
at-most-once dispatch within one retained ledger epoch, not exactly-once execution.
The run ID is untrusted output from the agent service. Named HTTP(S) routes use a
per-instance proxy with denial
limits of 30 per grant, 120 per tenant, and 480 per host per minute. Gateway rejects
the exact connector credential in response headers and the
decoded body stream, but configured upstreams remain trusted not to transform that
credential or disclose private origin details. Steward denies raw TCP/UDP, transparent
proxying, raw secrets, host mounts, Docker access, privileged mode, and undeclared
ports. The Executor HTTP API, `stewardctl node`, and `steward-mcp` call the same
Executor handlers.

Steward Control serves an embedded read-only React console at `/console/` on its
existing listener. It uses the same operator Bearer scope and exposes only the
operations summary, derived attention, nodes, command metadata, and credential
metadata. It has no mutation, private-signing, or secret-plaintext path. The
credential remains only in JavaScript memory and is cleared on lock, `pagehide`,
15 minutes idle, or an eight-hour absolute session; those browser timers do not
revoke the server-side bearer. Browser extensions remain trusted. The committed
bundle uses no CDN or telemetry endpoint, normal and air-gapped Go builds need no
npm, and maintainers reproduce it with lockfile-pinned React/Vite under Node.js 24
LTS.

Steward includes a qualified Hermes Agent adapter definition for upstream commit
`095b9eed3801c251796df93f48a8f2a527ff6e70` on `linux/amd64`; other platforms are
not yet qualified. The source-built image runs non-root
under gVisor, fixes inference through `http://steward-relay:8080/v1`, and exposes
bounded negotiation, health, run submission, and run-status operations on port
`8766`; run event streams are excluded. Qualification runs the signed, network-free
`steward.workspace-audit` skill, changed persisted state, and required a fresh
changed result after restart. It also required Hermes to discover and load the exact
signed `steward.connector-work` skill before demonstrating one authenticated effect,
replay and undeclared-operation denial, fixed-material secret scans, and a separate
Gateway receipt chain. The tenant-signed run path exercises a service-scoped key,
an owner-only exact-request bundle, generic task lifecycle commands, and offline task audit.
Steward ships the builder and disposable-host
acceptance harness but no prebuilt Hermes OCI archive because dependency and
base-image notices are incomplete. OpenClaw remains an unqualified layout contract.
